name: Embalse de la Ruidera desde Priego – circular por el río Guadiela
region_intro: Mountain biking around Priego de Córdoba offers diverse terrain within the Sierras Subbéticas Natural Park, a UNESCO Global Geopark. The region is characterized by a dramatic limestone Karst landscape, featuring rocky outcrops, steep mountains, and extensive olive groves. This geological formation provides a backdrop for mountain bike trails with significant elevation changes and varied surfaces. The area includes prominent peaks and numerous springs, contributing to a mix of challenging ascents and scenic routes. Best mountain bike trails around Priego The most popular mountain bike trail is Cerro Avanto – circular from Ca, a 18.0 miles (28.9 km) trail that takes 2 hours 54 minutes to complete. This difficult route features challenging ascents and panoramic views of the surrounding landscape. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Hoz del Conde, Escabas, and Guadiela Loop, a difficult 40.6 miles (65.3 km) path. This route traverses varied terrain, including sections with significant elevation gain through the Karst landscape. Local mountain bikers also love the Embalse de la Ruidera desde Priego – circular por el río Guadiela, a 20.1 miles (32.4 km) trail leading through areas near the Embalse de la Ruidera, often completed in about 2 hours 50 minutes. Mountain biking around Priego is defined by limestone Karst landscapes, steep mountains, and extensive olive groves. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Priego are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from 9 reviews. More than 60 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Priego's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around Priego de Córdoba?
A: The mountain bike trails around Priego de Córdoba are primarily characterized by a dramatic limestone Karst landscape within the Sierras Subbéticas Natural Park. You'll encounter rocky outcrops, steep mountains, and extensive olive groves. The terrain offers a mix of challenging ascents and descents, with routes ranging from moderate to difficult, providing varied surfaces for mountain biking.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike routes in the Priego de Córdoba area?
A: While many routes in the immediate Priego area feature significant elevation changes and are rated moderate to difficult, the wider Subbética region offers options like the &lt;strong&gt;Vía Verde del Aceite (Olive Oil Greenway)&lt;/strong&gt;. This disused railway line is converted for cyclists and hikers, providing a less strenuous and generally flatter option suitable for families looking to explore the countryside.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while mountain biking near Priego?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. Along the trails, you can encounter stunning geological formations typical of the UNESCO Global Geopark, including gorges and natural pools. Notable highlights include the dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5412048" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hoz del Conde&lt;/a&gt; gorge, the serene &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5404032" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cañamares natural pool&lt;/a&gt;, and the picturesque &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5404050" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfall of the Hocino Gorge&lt;/a&gt;. You might also pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5400732" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molino de Chincha Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: When is the best time of year for mountain biking in Priego de Córdoba?
A: The best times for mountain biking in Priego de Córdoba are typically during the spring (March-May) and autumn (September-November) months. During these periods, the weather is generally milder and more pleasant for outdoor activities, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the cooler, potentially wetter conditions of winter. The region's popularity for cycling peaks in months like March and September.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes available around Priego?
A: Yes, there are several circular mountain bike routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017414662/embalse-de-la-ruidera-desde-priego-circular-por-el-rio-guadiela" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de la Ruidera desde Priego – circular por el río Guadiela&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 32.4 km trail that takes about 2 hours 50 minutes. Another option is the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1016881236/cerro-avanto-circular-from-ca" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Avanto – circular from Ca&lt;/a&gt;, an 28.9 km route with significant ascents.

Q: What is the overall difficulty level of mountain bike trails in Priego de Córdoba?
A: The mountain bike trails in Priego de Córdoba generally range from moderate to difficult. Out of the 27 available tours, 17 are rated moderate and 10 are difficult. These routes often feature significant elevation gains, with some difficult trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017229721/hoz-del-conde-escabas-and-guadiela-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hoz del Conde, Escabas, and Guadiela Loop&lt;/a&gt; involving over 1200 meters of ascent over 65 km.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Priego de Córdoba?
A: The mountain bike trails around Priego are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from 9 reviews. Over 60 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the challenging ascents, panoramic views, and the unique limestone Karst landscapes.

Q: Are there options for longer, more challenging mountain bike tours in the area?
A: Absolutely. For experienced mountain bikers seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017229721/hoz-del-conde-escabas-and-guadiela-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hoz del Conde, Escabas, and Guadiela Loop&lt;/a&gt; offer a difficult 65.3 km ride with over 1200 meters of elevation gain. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1016915277/carrascosa-hoz-somera-and-molino-de-chincha-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carrascosa, Hoz Somera and Molino de Chincha Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 41.3 km with over 1100 meters of climbing.

Q: Can I find mountain bike trails that offer scenic viewpoints in Priego?
A: Yes, the dramatic Karst landscape and high peaks in the Sierras Subbéticas Natural Park provide numerous opportunities for scenic viewpoints. The region is known for its 'Balcón de Andalucía' (Andalusia's balcony) nickname, particularly around the Sierra de Cabra. Many difficult routes feature challenging ascents that reward riders with panoramic views of the surrounding mountains and olive groves.

Q: Are there any trails that pass by water features like rivers or reservoirs?
A: Yes, several routes incorporate water features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017414662/embalse-de-la-ruidera-desde-priego-circular-por-el-rio-guadiela" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de la Ruidera desde Priego – circular por el río Guadiela&lt;/a&gt; trail, for instance, leads through areas near the Embalse de la Ruidera and along the Guadiela River. The region also features the Iznájar Reservoir, Andalusia's largest, and the River Genil, which offer significant aquatic environments.

Q: How many mountain bike trails are there in total around Priego de Córdoba?
A: There are over 25 mountain bike trails available around Priego de Córdoba, offering a variety of experiences within the Sierras Subbéticas Natural Park. These routes cater to different skill levels, predominantly moderate to difficult, and allow you to explore the region's unique Karst landscape and olive groves.
tips: Nice and very quiet place on the banks of the Guadiela river as it passes through the Las Praderas area. Ideal place for a break on summer days.
The forest track that runs next to the Guadiela river offers beautiful views of the Ruidera reservoir over the course of this river. An ideal place to watch the sunsets.
